hle.canonical_pass_notes: White background, black text reading 'All tests passed'. Confirmed visually 2026-04-07 from NBA + SkyEmu cross-agreement (both produce the same hash and the same image). Two independent emulators converging on the all-pass output is strong evidence this is the test ROM's canonical pass state.

nanoboyadvance.notes: Matches canonical pass hash. Cross-confirmed by SkyEmu which produced an identical hash.

skyemu.notes: Matches canonical pass hash. Cross-confirmed by NBA which produced an identical hash.

mgba.notes: Expected/known failure. Displays 'Failed test 522' on its in-test failure screen. mgba has a real bug at jsmolka instruction-test #522 in HLE BIOS mode. The hash should remain stable across mgba runs; a change in this hash is a regression to investigate.

official.canonical_pass_notes: All tests passed. NBA + skyemu agree under official BIOS.

mgba.notes: Same hash as HLE - mgba renders Failed test 522 under both modes.

cleanroom.canonical_pass_notes: All tests passed. NBA + skyemu agree under cleanroom.

mgba.notes: Same Failed test 522 hash as HLE/official.

hle.canonical_pass_notes: White background, black text 'All tests passed'. Pixel-identical to the jsmolka-arm canonical pass hash because the same render template is used. Cross-confirmed by NBA + SkyEmu agreement.

mgba.notes: Documented mgba HLE bug at jsmolka Thumb instruction-test #230. Renders 'Failed test 230' correctly. Hash is identical to cable_club's — both emulators reach the same broken state on the same instruction.

cable_club.notes: Cable Club fails Thumb instruction-test #230 with the same bug as mgba — bit-identical hash. This is a known-cross-emulator failure (both implementations share the same incorrect behavior on this Thumb encoding). IMPORTANT: cable_club's text rendering WORKS CORRECTLY on this test ('Failed test 230' is fully legible), which means the rendering bug observed on jsmolka-arm is specific to that ROM's code path, not a generic cable_club PPU bug. Bug investigation should focus on what jsmolka-arm does differently.

official.canonical_pass_notes: Re-promoted from 2-runner consensus; previous canonical was 59ce42abae9825c2.

mgba.notes: Identical to HLE - bit-identical to cable_club.

cleanroom.canonical_pass_notes: Re-promoted from 2-runner consensus; previous canonical was 59ce42abae9825c2.

hle.canonical_pass_notes: 'All tests passed'. Only mgba's HLE BIOS is complete enough to pass all jsmolka BIOS tests under HLE mode. NBA, SkyEmu, and cable_club all fail at BIOS test 001 — an SWI handler that the other 3 emulators have not fully implemented in HLE.

mgba.notes: mgba's HLE BIOS is the most complete in the field. Passes all jsmolka BIOS tests.

nanoboyadvance.notes: Fails at BIOS test 001. NBA has no true HLE BIOS — it uses real BIOS data with --skip-bios for boot. The test it fails is whatever SWI handler is exercised first.

skyemu.notes: Fails at BIOS test 001 — bit-identical hash to NBA and cable_club. SkyEmu's HLE fallback BIOS is the Cult-of-GBA replacement, which fails this specific SWI.

cable_club.notes: All tests passed. Fixed by initialising the BIOS open-bus latch to 0xE129F000, setting it to 0xE3A02004 after every HLE SWI return, 0xE25EF004 on HLE IRQ entry (so the user ISR sees the trampoline-prefetch value test 003 expects), 0xE55EC002 after the HLE IRQ trampoline's SUBS PC,LR,#4 at 0x13C, AND clearing CPSR.I in HLE skip-boot so VBlank IRQs actually fire (without that test 003's user ISR was never invoked). Joins mgba as the second emulator passing jsmolka-bios under HLE.

official.canonical_pass_notes: All tests passed. NBA and skyemu now pass under official BIOS (joining mgba) - they're using the real Nintendo BIOS instead of their HLE/Cult-of-GBA fallbacks. cable_club is the LONE FAILURE under official mode - cable_club's BIOS execution path has a bug that does NOT depend on which BIOS bytes are loaded. See .agents/handoff/20260407-jsmolka-bios-cable_club-swi-dispatch-bug.md.

cable_club.notes: All tests passed. Cable Club now treats every real-BIOS mode the same as HLE skip-boot (PC=0x08000000, BIOS bytes loaded for read-protection latches, HLE SWI/IRQ handlers, install_irq_trampoline always installed). The standalone real-BIOS-execution path (cpu.use_real_bios=true) is still WIP. Same canonical hash as mgba, NBA, and skyemu.

nanoboyadvance.notes: Now passes under official BIOS (was fail under HLE).

skyemu.notes: Now passes under official BIOS (was fail under HLE).

cleanroom.canonical_pass_notes: Re-promoted from 3-runner consensus; previous canonical was 59ce42abae9825c2.

cable_club.notes: All tests passed. The earlier 'cleanroom mode broken' state (Cult-of-GBA boot screen, hash 22de4108) is fixed: runner.rs now ALWAYS install_irq_trampoline()s and jumps PC to 0x08000000 in real-BIOS mode regardless of which BIOS bytes were loaded, so the cart actually runs. With the BIOS-latch fixes from the HLE pass, all 13 BIOS tests now succeed under cleanroom too.

mgba.notes: Failed test 001 — under cleanroom, mgba uses Cult-of-GBA's BIOS bytes which lacks the SWI handler test 001 needs.

nanoboyadvance.notes: Failed test 001 — same hash as mgba and skyemu cleanroom.

skyemu.notes: Failed test 001 — same hash. SkyEmu's internal fallback IS Cult-of-GBA so this matches its HLE result.

hle.canonical_pass_notes: All four runners (cable_club, mgba, NBA, skyemu) hit the canonical 'All tests passed' image. 4-way consensus pass for jsmolka-memory under HLE BIOS — strong cross-emulator agreement that all four implementations correctly handle the memory map / mirroring / access widths covered by this test.

cleanroom.canonical_pass_notes: All tests passed. mgba+NBA+skyemu 3-way consensus pass under cleanroom. cable_club bug.

hle.canonical_pass_notes: All four runners hit the canonical pass image. 4-way consensus pass.
Re-promoted from 2-runner consensus; previous canonical was 59ce42abae9825c2.

skyemu.notes: Lone passer. SkyEmu's UB handling matches jsmolka's expected output.

cable_club.notes: Fails at test 002. Bit-identical hash to mgba — both implementations converge on the same incorrect UB result.

mgba.notes: Fails at test 002. Bit-identical hash to cable_club.

nanoboyadvance.notes: Fails at test 001 — earlier failure than cable_club/mgba. NBA's UB handling diverges from both the lone-passer (skyemu) and the cable_club+mgba pair.

official.canonical_pass_notes: Re-promoted from 2-runner consensus; previous canonical was 59ce42abae9825c2.

mgba.notes: Same as HLE - bit-identical to cable_club.

cleanroom.canonical_pass_notes: Re-promoted from 2-runner consensus; previous canonical was 59ce42abae9825c2.

hle.canonical_pass_notes: All tests passed. cable_club is the LONE PASSER on jsmolka-flash128 under HLE. Cable Club flash emulation is the most-tested in the field (FireRed saves work end-to-end), and this test exposes corner cases the other three runners do not handle. mgba, NBA, and skyemu each fail at different points.

cable_club.notes: Lone passer. Test ROM expected output for the 128 KB Macronix flash chip path.

mgba.notes: Different failure than NBA and skyemu.

nanoboyadvance.notes: Different failure than mgba and skyemu.

skyemu.notes: Different failure than mgba and NBA. Three-way divergence among failing runners.

official.canonical_pass_notes: Same as HLE. cable_club lone passer.

cleanroom.canonical_pass_notes: NO PASSING RUNNER under cleanroom. cable_club (the lone passer under HLE/official) is in its broken cleanroom state. mgba/NBA/skyemu each fail differently.

hle.canonical_pass_notes: All tests passed. cable_club lone passer; same architectural reason as flash128 - Cable Club flash chip emulation is more accurate than the field on jsmolka corner cases.

cable_club.notes: Lone passer. Same hash and image as cable_club flash128 result.

mgba.notes: Same hash as mgba flash128 failure - mgba fails both flash sizes the same way.

nanoboyadvance.notes: Same hash as NBA flash128 failure - NBA fails both sizes identically.

skyemu.notes: Different hash from flash128 - skyemu flash64 vs flash128 failures diverge.

official.canonical_pass_notes: Same as HLE. cable_club lone passer.

hle.canonical_pass_notes: All tests passed. cable_club + mgba both pass jsmolka-sram (2-way consensus). NBA and skyemu fail differently.

official.canonical_pass_notes: Same as HLE. cable_club + mgba pass.

cleanroom.canonical_pass_notes: Lone passer is mgba (cable_club is broken under cleanroom; under HLE/official cable_club joins mgba).

hle.canonical_pass_notes: All tests passed. 3-way consensus pass (cable_club, mgba, NBA). SkyEmu is the lone failure. Tests the no-save-chip code path.

skyemu.notes: Lone failure among the four runners.

cleanroom.canonical_pass_notes: mgba+NBA pass; cable_club is broken; skyemu fails.

hle.canonical_pass_notes: Black background with white 'Hello world!' text rendered as a tile pattern. Visually verified - text is legible and correct. 4-way pixel-identical agreement.
Dark-to-bright blue horizontal gradient with 16 discrete shade bands. Visually verified. 4-way pixel-identical agreement.

Vertical alternating blue stripes. cable_club + mgba + NBA produce the bit-identical correct pattern (3-way consensus). SkyEmu produces a visually-similar pattern with a subtle pixel difference at the top edge - likely a one-line scroll offset or color quantization difference in SkyEmu output path. Recognizable but not bit-identical.

skyemu.notes: Visually similar (correct stripe pattern) but bit-different - one-line offset or color difference at the top edge.

official.canonical_pass_notes: Same as HLE - 3-way pass plus skyemu near-pass.

cleanroom.canonical_pass_notes: Vertical stripe pattern. mgba+NBA bit-identical pass; skyemu near-pass; cable_club broken.

skyemu.notes: Near-pass — pixel difference at top edge.
